admission.discharge_condition: Stable. Afebrile. Pain relieved. Voiding normally. DJ stent in situ (left side). No active haematuria.

clinical.presenting_complaints: Severe colicky RIGHT flank pain radiating to right groin and inner thigh for 4 days. Associated nausea, vomiting, burning micturition and increased urinary frequency. Microscopic haematuria noted on day 2. No fever. No urinary retention.
clinical.initial_assessment: Mr. Selvam Rajakumar, a 38-year-old male, presents with a 4-day history of severe colicky RIGHT flank pain radiating to the right groin and inner thigh — characteristic of a lower ureteric calculus. Pain is intermittent and severe. Associated nausea, vomiting, burning micturition and urinary frequency suggest distal ureteric irritation. Microscopic haematuria noted. No fever, no urinary retention. USG abdomen and CT KUB both confirm a 9mm calculus in the RIGHT lower ureter at the vesicoureteric junction (VUJ) with mild RIGHT hydronephrosis. LEFT kidney and ureter entirely normal. No previous history of renal or ureteric stones. Referred for right ureteroscopy and stone removal under PM-JAY. Clinical Examination Findings: Conscious, cooperative, in mild discomfort. No pallor or icterus. Weight / Height 72 kg / 168 cm. Vitals BP 128/82 mmHg, Pulse 86/min, SpO2 98% (room air), Temp 98.8 F, RR 16/min. CVS S1 S2 heard, no murmurs. Respiratory Air entry bilateral, clear. Abdomen Soft, mild RIGHT flank and right iliac fossa tenderness. RIGHT renal angle tenderness POSITIVE. LEFT renal angle tenderness NEGATIVE. CNS No focal neurological deficits. Local RIGHT flank tenderness. RIGHT renal angle tenderness — positive. LEFT side — no tenderness. External genitalia normal.
clinical.significant_past_history: No significant past medical history. No previous renal or ureteric stones. No known drug allergies. No previous surgeries. Chronic Illnesses None, Previous Surgeries None, Current Medications None, Known Allergies None, Smoking / Alcohol Non-smoker, social alcohol.
clinical.primary_diagnosis_at_admission: Right Lower Ureteric Calculus 9mm at Vesicoureteric Junction (VUJ) with Mild Right Hydronephrosis
clinical.final_diagnosis_at_discharge: Post-procedure status following successful left ureteroscopy with holmium laser lithotripsy and stone removal at left VUJ
clinical.key_investigations: Pre-operative Blood Reports (CBC, RFT, Electrolytes, PT/INR, Sugar), Urine Routine and Culture Report, ECG Report, USG Abdomen & Pelvis Report, CT KUB Report, X-ray KUB (post-procedure).
clinical.investigation_findings: Haemoglobin (Hb) 14.2 g/dL (Normal), WBC Count 9,800 /cumm (Normal), Platelet Count 2.6 Lakh (Normal), Serum Creatinine 1.1 mg/dL (Normal), Urea 32 mg/dL (Normal), Electrolytes Na 138, K 4.2 mEq/L (Normal), PT / INR 13.2 sec / 1.05 (Normal), Blood Sugar Random 102 mg/dL (Normal). Urine Routine: Microscopic haematuria — RBC 15-20/hpf. Pus cells 2-4/hpf. No casts. Urine Culture: No growth (Normal). ECG: Normal sinus rhythm. USG Abdomen & Pelvis: RIGHT lower ureteric calculus 9mm at VUJ with mild RIGHT hydronephrosis. LEFT kidney and ureter normal. CT KUB: RIGHT lower ureteric calculus at VUJ measuring 9x7mm — distal ureter just proximal to bladder. Mild RIGHT hydronephrosis. LEFT ureter and kidney ENTIRELY NORMAL. Post-procedure X-ray KUB: No residual calculus seen. DJ stent in situ — left side.
clinical.treatment_given: Left Ureteroscopy with Holmium Laser Lithotripsy and Stone Removal — Lower Ureter (VUJ) performed under Spinal Anaesthesia. Flexible ureteroscopy performed via left ureteric orifice. 9x7mm calculus identified at left VUJ. Holmium laser lithotripsy performed — stone fragmented to dust and small fragments. Fragments retrieved with stone basket. DJ stent placed left side. Complete stone clearance confirmed on fluoroscopy. Post-procedure: Patient comfortable, vitals stable. Oral fluids tolerated. IV antibiotics given. Urinary catheter removed after 4 hours. Patient voiding normally. No haematuria.
clinical.operative_findings: Left lower ureteric calculus 9x7mm at VUJ confirmed. Stone fragmented and cleared. No ureteric injury. No bleeding.
clinical.final_procedure_performed: Left Ureteroscopy with Holmium Laser Lithotripsy and Stone Removal — Lower Ureter (VUJ)
clinical.advice_on_discharge: Day 7 (10/07/2026): Review at Urology OPD — DJ stent removal planning. Day 14 (17/07/2026): DJ stent removal under local anaesthesia. Increase fluid intake to 3 litres per day to prevent stone recurrence. Warning Signs: Report immediately for high fever (>101F), severe pain, inability to pass urine, or heavy bleeding in urine.

Apollo Speciality Hospital

DISCHARGE SUMMARY
Summary No: APH/DS/2026/TCNEW03 Date: 03/07/2026
SECTION 1 — HOSPITAL & PATIENT INFORMATION
Hospital Name Apollo Speciality Hospital
Address 21, Greams Road, Chennai - 600006, Tamil Nadu
PMJAY Empanelment ID H001
Patient Name Selvam Rajakumar
Age / Gender 38 Years / Male
PMJAY Beneficiary ID P003
PMJAY Card No. TN-PMJAY-2024-003891
Address 7, Nehru Street, Villivakkam, Chennai - 600049
SECTION 2 — ADMISSION & DISCHARGE DETAILS
Date & Time of Admission 02/07/2026 at 09:00 hrs
Date & Time of Discharge 03/07/2026 at 11:00 hrs
Total Duration of Stay 1 Day (Day Care)
Type of Admission Planned / Elective
Ward / Room Category General Ward
Treating Consultant Dr. Ramesh Natarajan
Qualification MBBS, MS, MCh (Urology)
Registration Number TN-MED-38892
Specialty Urology
PMJAY Package Booked SU014A
Pre-Auth Reference PMJAY-PREAUTH-2026-TCNEW03-001
SECTION 3 — CLINICAL SUMMARY
Presenting Complaints:
Severe colicky left flank pain radiating to left groin and inner thigh for 4 days. Associated nausea, vomiting, burning
micturition. Microscopic haematuria.
Relevant Medical History:
No significant past medical history. No previous renal or ureteric stones. No known drug allergies. No previous
surgeries.
Pre-operative Examination:
BP 128/82 mmHg, Pulse 86/min, SpO2 98%, afebrile. LEFT renal angle tenderness positive. RIGHT renal angle
tenderness negative. USG and CT KUB confirmed left lower ureteric calculus 9x7mm at left VUJ.

SECTION 4 — DIAGNOSIS
Provisional Diagnosis (Admission) Left Lower Ureteric Calculus 9mm at Vesicoureteric Junction (VUJ) with Mild Left
Hydronephrosis — ICD-10: N20.1
Final Diagnosis (Discharge) Post-procedure status following successful left ureteroscopy with holmium laser
lithotripsy and stone removal at left VUJ — ICD-10: N20.1
SECTION 5 — PROCEDURE PERFORMED
Procedure Name Left Ureteroscopy with Holmium Laser Lithotripsy and Stone Removal — Lower
Ureter (VUJ)
PMJAY Package SU014A
Laterality LEFT side — Unilateral
Date of Procedure 02/07/2026 at 11:00 hrs
Duration 45 minutes
Anaesthesia Spinal Anaesthesia — Dr. Anitha Kumar, MD Anaesthesiology, Reg: TN-MED-29341
Technique Flexible ureteroscopy performed via left ureteric orifice. 9x7mm calculus identified at
left VUJ. Holmium laser lithotripsy performed — stone fragmented to dust and small
fragments. Fragments retrieved with stone basket. DJ stent placed left side.
Complete stone clearance confirmed on fluoroscopy.
Intraoperative Findings Left lower ureteric calculus 9x7mm at VUJ confirmed. Stone fragmented and cleared.
No ureteric injury. No bleeding.
Post-procedure Imaging X-ray KUB post-procedure: No residual calculus seen. DJ stent in situ — left side.
SECTION 6 — POST-OPERATIVE COURSE
Post-procedure: Patient comfortable, vitals stable. Oral fluids tolerated. IV antibiotics given. Urinary catheter removed
after 4 hours. Patient voiding normally. No haematuria. Declared fit for discharge.
SECTION 7 — COMPLICATIONS
No intraoperative or post-procedure complications.
SECTION 8 — CONDITION AT DISCHARGE
Stable. Afebrile. Pain relieved. Voiding normally. DJ stent in situ (left side). No active haematuria.
SECTION 9 — DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS
# Medication Dose Frequency Duration
1 Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500mg 1 tablet Twice daily 5 Days
2 Tab. Tamsulosin 0.4mg 1 tablet Once daily at night Until stent removal
3 Tab. Paracetamol 500mg 1-2 tablets As needed 5 Days
4 Tab. Pantoprazole 40mg 1 tablet Once daily 5 Days
5 Syp. Potassium Citrate 10 ml Three times daily 1 Month

SECTION 10 — FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS
Day 7 (10/07/2026): Review at Urology OPD — DJ stent removal planning.
Day 14 (17/07/2026): DJ stent removal under local anaesthesia.
Increase fluid intake to 3 litres per day to prevent stone recurrence.
Warning Signs: Report immediately for high fever (>101F), severe pain, inability to pass urine, or heavy bleeding in
urine.
SECTION 11 — DOCUMENTS SUBMITTED WITH THIS DISCHARGE SUMMARY
